summary: react-jsonwebtoken typosquats jsonwebtoken and executes remote code on require
details: Package name and metadata impersonate the widely used `jsonwebtoken` library (author attributed to `auth0`). On require, `decode.js` reconstructs a base64-obfuscated URL (`jsonkeeper.com/b/0SZDF`), issues an outbound HTTPS GET, and passes the response body to `new Function.constructor('require', errCode)`, then invokes it with the local `require`. This gives the remote host arbitrary code execution in the installer's Node process on every load. The destination is a mutable pastebin-style host, so the delivered payload can change at any time. Obfuscation of the C2 host and path via base64 further indicates hostile intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
